issues.title: Client certificates
issues.author: yaronf
issues.body:
From EKR:

In S 3.1 you say that this can be used for both server authentication
and client authentication, but the setting isn't really symmetrical.
S 3.2 says:

   *  The peer SHOULD include the cached algorithm in the
      signature_algorithms extension of its ClientHello (or
      CertificateRequest for servers), and MUST NOT include legacy (non-
      PQC) algorithms.

I don't see how this works: the client (usually) knows the server's
identity in advance but the server usually does not know the client's
in advance, so how does it know whether it has cached information or
not prior to receiving the client's certificate. One might attempt to
resolve this by just saying that the server should send its ordinary
signature_algorithms and then reject any non-PQ algorithms from the
client, but I don't think that quite works as-is because the client
might initially prefer PQ and then later not prefer PQ leading to a
failure. At least, I think you would need a warning that you shouldn't
advertise this extension as a client unless you are committing to
always sending a PQ cert if possible.

reviews.author: yaronf
reviews.body: Looks good, other than one comment.

comments.body: I prefer the original text here, because a client may connect through a MITM for a few times, but once it connects directly even once, it obtains correct information.

pulls.title: Language consistency improvements
pulls.author: yaronf
pulls.body:
- Standardize terminology: use "PQC" consistently instead of "PQ"
- Standardize MitM capitalization (was "MiTM")
- Fix field name references: use `algorithm_validity_period` consistently
- Add code formatting to `pq_cert_available` identifier
- Fix RFC 2119 keyword: change "should" to "SHOULD"
- Add missing comma in abstract after "TLS layer"
- Improve clarity: "this extension" instead of "the extension"
- Complete IANA section: add "registry." to request sentence
- Fix IANA table spacing: "CH, CR, CT" (was "CH, CR,CT")

pulls.title: Technical consistency improvements
pulls.author: yaronf
pulls.body:
## Summary

This PR addresses technical consistency issues identified in the draft:

- **Bidirectional scope clarification**: Abstract and introduction now explicitly state the mechanism works peer-to-peer, with typical use being client-caching-server but also supporting server-caching-client
- **Cache update logic**: Clarified that when switching algorithms, use the new validity period from the extension
- **Zero value semantics**: Defined that `algorithm_validity_period` of zero means don't cache (if no cache exists) or clear cache (if exists)
- **Validation requirements**: Specified that `signature_algorithm` must match certificate and must be a PQC algorithm
- **Extension presence logic**: Clarified that when using traditional (non-PQC) certificate, sender should send extension with no data to indicate support
- **Commitment timing**: Clarified that duration is measured from the current TLS handshake

## Changes

All changes improve clarity and consistency without changing the fundamental protocol design.
